meaning: v.再点火,再点燃,重新激起
examples: You may need to reignite the pilot light.
The oven burners reignite automatically if blown out.
The presidents will meet to try and diffuse the tensions that threaten to reignite the conflict.
Currently, the U.S. savings rate hovers at 6%, so these cuts may reignite consumption.
Dr Schmidt said the UK needed to reignite children's passion for science, engineering and maths.
